questions.title: Which artifact types get report-style validation in v1?
questions.context: Existing admission gates already validate intent (packages/intent), capability envelope (packages/intent/capability-admission.ts), plan (packages/planning), repo scope (packages/policy/src/admission), and change-set (apply-change-set / Phase 12 invariant). Each stage today is fail-on-first or partial.

options.label: Intent + Plan only
options.description: Author-time UX where errors hurt most; defer capability/repo-policy/change-set to a later pass.

options.label: Intent + Plan + Capability Envelope + Repo Policy
options.description: All author-edited artifacts. Skips machine-derived change-sets.

options.label: All five (incl. change-set)
options.description: Maximum coverage; aligns with Phase 12 apply-change-set invariant work and Phase 13 replay-with-edit.

options.label: Custom subset
options.description: Specify in chat-more.

questions.title: Author-time vs admission-time vs both?
questions.context: Author-time = `protostar validate <artifact>` returns a report on a file the operator is editing. Admission-time = the existing gate runs and emits its decision. CONTEXT stub says reports compose with gates, but the *invocation surface* is still open.

options.label: Author-time only
options.description: New CLI surface. Admission gates keep their current decision artifacts unchanged.

options.label: Both — gate decision wraps the same report
options.description: Admission gate outcome derives from `report.blockers.length === 0`. Single source of truth, but requires refactoring existing decision artifacts.

options.label: Both — separate but consistent
options.description: Admission gates keep their existing decision artifacts; author-time validate emits a report. The same Finding shape is used in both, but persistence is separate.

questions.title: Migration vs. greenfield Finding type?
questions.context: `packages/intent/src/admission-decision.ts` already has `IntentAdmissionPolicyFinding`, `IntentAdmissionMissingFieldDetection`, `IntentAdmissionHardZeroReason`, `IntentAdmissionRequiredClarification`. `packages/intent/src/capability-admission.ts` uses a `findings` array with `severity: 'block' | 'ambiguity'`, `code`, `fieldPath`, `message`. Phase 14 either unifies or layers on top.

options.label: Unify — refactor existing findings to one Finding type
options.description: Single Finding shape across intent, capability, plan, repo, change-set. Largest blast radius but cleanest endgame.

options.label: Layer — adapter converts existing findings to Report at the boundary
options.description: Existing types unchanged; `@protostar/validation` package owns adapter layer that produces unified Report from heterogeneous inputs.

options.label: Greenfield — new Finding type for new validators only; existing gates untouched
options.description: Lowest blast radius; but coexistence may confuse operators (two finding shapes).

questions.title: Anchor format for findings
questions.context: Findings need to point at *where* in the artifact the issue is. Existing capability-admission uses dotted `fieldPath` like `capabilityEnvelope.pnpm.allowedAdds.0`. Plans have IDs like `01-09-parameterized-admission-e2e`.

options.label: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901)
options.description: Standard, tooling-friendly (`/capabilityEnvelope/pnpm/allowedAdds/0`). Less readable for plans.

options.label: Dotted path
options.description: Matches existing fieldPath convention. Ambiguous for keys with dots.

options.label: Domain anchor (typed)
options.description: Discriminated union: `{ kind: 'jsonPath', value }`, `{ kind: 'planId', value }`, `{ kind: 'fileLine', file, line }`. Most expressive, most code.

options.label: Hybrid — JSON Pointer for fields + domain anchor for plans/files
options.description: Pragmatic; one pointer type per artifact category.

questions.title: Multi-anchor per finding?
questions.context: Some findings reference multiple locations (e.g. 'plan 03 wave 2 conflicts with plan 05 wave 1').

options.label: Single anchor per finding
options.description: Simpler schema; cross-reference findings duplicate.

options.label: Primary anchor + optional `relatedAnchors[]`
options.description: Render renders primary; related cross-link in UI/CLI.

options.label: `anchors[]` array (1..N)
options.description: Most flexible; renderer must pick a primary heuristically.

questions.title: Severity levels
questions.context: CONTEXT stub asks: just `blocker | warning`, or add `info | suggestion`? Existing capability findings use `block | ambiguity`. Existing intent has `policyFindings`, `requiredClarifications`, `hardZeroReasons` — three implicit severities.

options.label: Two levels: blocker, warning
options.description: Simplest. Maps cleanly to gate outcome (blockers === 0 → admit).

options.label: Three levels: blocker, warning, info
options.description: info = render-only, no admission impact. Useful for 'tip: this could be tighter'.

options.label: Four levels: blocker, warning, info, suggestion
options.description: suggestion = actionable proposal with remediation hint. Larger schema, may rot if not used.

options.label: Two + 'overridable' flag on warnings
options.description: Models existing capability `ambiguity` severity which has an `overridden` flag.

questions.title: Finding required fields
questions.context: Beyond anchor + severity + message, what's required? Existing findings already have `code` (stable identifier).

options.label: code, severity, anchor, message, validatorId, validatorVersion
options.description: Minimum for determinism + plugin attribution + machine grouping.

options.label: Add: remediationHint (string), docsUrl (string)
options.description: Author-experience win; adds schema surface to maintain.

options.label: Add: suggestedFix (structured patch)
options.description: Enables auto-fix UX (Phase 13 replay-with-edit synergy). Significantly larger scope.

options.label: All of the above (a + b + c)
options.description: Largest schema; richest UX.

questions.title: Report schema versioning model
questions.context: Existing artifacts use single envelope `schemaVersion` (e.g. `protostar.intent.admission-decision.v1`).

options.label: Single envelope `schemaVersion` per report
options.description: Matches existing convention. Validator versions live inside per-finding fields.

options.label: Envelope version + per-finding schema version
options.description: Allows individual finding shapes to evolve independently. More plumbing.

options.label: Envelope version + content hash of registered validator set
options.description: Strongest determinism guarantee — replays detect validator drift.

questions.title: Where does validation logic live?
questions.context: Authority boundary today: only `apps/factory-cli` and `packages/repo` may do filesystem I/O. New `@protostar/validation` package would be pure (no I/O), wrapping per-stage validators.

options.label: New `@protostar/validation` package (pure, no I/O)
options.description: Owns Finding + Report types + plugin registry + report runner. Existing per-stage validators register into it.

options.label: Each stage package owns its validators; `@protostar/validation` is just types
options.description: Smallest footprint; intent owns intent validators, planning owns plan validators, etc.

options.label: Subprocess plugins (capability-bounded)
options.description: External validators run as child processes through repo runner. Maximum extensibility — and maximum complexity. Probably 1.0 work.

questions.title: Plugin registration mechanism
questions.context: How does a validator get into the registry?

options.label: Explicit import + `register(validator)` call
options.description: Boring, type-safe, no magic. Each app builds its own validator set.

options.label: Manifest in package.json (`protostar.validators: [...]`)
options.description: Discoverable via workspace scan. Adds runtime parsing. Existing `protostar.tier` precedent (Phase 12).

options.label: Directory convention scan
options.description: Magic / brittle. Don't recommend but listing for completeness.

questions.title: Validator interface shape
questions.context: What does a validator look like at the type level?

options.label: Pure function: `(artifact) => Finding[]`
options.description: Simplest. Validator owns its own parsing/typing.

options.label: `{ id, version, target: SchemaName, validate: (parsed) => Finding[] }`
options.description: Registry pre-parses artifact by `target` then dispatches. Type-safe by target.

options.label: Class with lifecycle: init/validate/dispose
options.description: Allows expensive setup (e.g. compiled regex caches). Overkill for v1.

questions.title: Validator ordering / dependencies
questions.context: Some validators may want to skip if a structural one already failed (e.g. don't run policy checks on an unparseable plan).

options.label: Independent — all validators run regardless
options.description: Maximum information per pass. May produce noisy reports for malformed inputs.

options.label: Two phases: structural → semantic; semantic skipped if structural blocks
options.description: Matches existing intent admission staging (parse → ambiguity → policy).

options.label: Explicit DAG via `dependsOn: validatorId[]`
options.description: Most flexible; harder to reason about.

questions.title: Determinism contract
questions.context: CONTEXT stub success criterion: same input + pinned validators → byte-identical report.

options.label: Pure functions only — no I/O, no clock, no random
options.description: Enforced by tier (`pure` / `no-net` / `no-fs` contracts already exist in Phase 1/12). Tested via contract test.

options.label: Pure + allow read-only reference data (e.g. allowlists from a snapshot file)
options.description: Reference data hashed into report so determinism holds.

options.label: Best-effort; no enforcement
options.description: Risk of replay drift. Don't recommend given Phase 13 dependency.

questions.title: Gate ↔ report relationship
questions.context: Existing admission posture is fail-closed default-DENY (Phase 2 lock). Phase 14 must preserve it.

options.label: Gate outcome derives from report: `admitted = report.blockers.length === 0`
options.description: Single source of truth. Requires per-stage refactor — biggest blast radius but cleanest.

options.label: Gate runs independently; report is a separate parallel artifact
options.description: Lowest blast radius. Risk: drift between gate decision and report.

options.label: Gate runs report internally then projects to its existing decision shape
options.description: Hybrid — internal unification, external compatibility. Per-stage opt-in.

questions.title: Warning semantics
questions.context: Reports surface warnings. Admission gates today are binary admit/refuse.

options.label: Warnings are advisory only — never affect admission
options.description: Author-experience signal; gate ignores them.

options.label: Default advisory + opt-in strict mode flag (`--strict-warnings`) promotes to blockers
options.description: Useful for CI; requires flag plumbing.

options.label: Per-validator declares `warningPolicy: 'advisory' | 'strict-in-ci'`
options.description: Validator knows best. Adds schema field.

questions.title: Default-DENY preservation contract test
questions.context: Phase 2 locks default-DENY repo policy. Phase 14 must prove it.

options.label: Required — `gate-report-parity.contract.test.ts` in `@protostar/admission-e2e`
options.description: Asserts: empty validator set → gate still refuses (no implicit allow). Composes with Phase 12 BOUNDARY-06 pattern.

options.label: Defer — covered transitively by existing admission tests
options.description: Smaller scope; weaker guarantee.

questions.title: Where do reports live?
questions.context: Existing convention: `.protostar/runs/<id>/` for run-bundle artifacts (Phase 9). Author-time `validate` may run outside a run.

options.label: `.protostar/runs/<id>/validation/<artifact>.report.json` (in run bundle only)
options.description: Aligns with existing layout. Author-time `validate` outside a run prints to stdout, no persistence.

options.label: Run-bundle path + `.protostar/validation/<hash>.report.json` for ad-hoc
options.description: Author-time ad-hoc reports content-addressed for cache + diff.

options.label: Inline in admission decision artifact
options.description: Single file per gate. No author-time persistence layer.

options.label: stdout only for author-time; inline in admission decision for gate-time
options.description: Minimum new persistence surface.

questions.title: Report immutability + indexing
questions.context: Run bundles are append-only / content-addressed today.

options.label: Append-only, content-addressed by report hash; no per-run index
options.description: Matches existing run-bundle posture. Replay diffs by hash.

options.label: Append-only + per-run `validation-index.jsonl` (one line per report)
options.description: Faster lookup; needs writer in factory-cli (authority boundary).

options.label: Mutable file overwritten on re-validate
options.description: Conflicts with Phase 13 replay determinism. Don't recommend.

questions.title: CLI surface for `validate`
questions.context: Phase 9 added `protostar-factory` subcommands via commander. Phase 14 needs an entrypoint.

options.label: `protostar-factory validate <path>`
options.description: Auto-detects artifact type by content/schemaVersion. One subcommand.

options.label: `protostar-factory validate <type> <path>` (e.g. `validate intent ./draft.json`)
options.description: Explicit, no detection magic; predictable error messages.

options.label: `protostar-factory <type> validate <path>` (subgroup per artifact type)
options.description: Mirrors `git remote add` style. More commands to register.

questions.title: Render groupings + output formats
questions.context: Operator reads the report; CI/automation reads JSON.

options.label: JSON only; humans pipe to a renderer
options.description: Smallest surface. Matches dark-factory posture (no progress logs).

options.label: JSON + human renderer (`--format human`) grouped by anchor path
options.description: Default JSON; human view on demand. Anchor grouping matches 'fix one file at a time' UX.

options.label: JSON + human grouped by severity (blockers first, then warnings)
options.description: Fix-blockers-first UX.

options.label: JSON + human + SARIF (`--format sarif`)
options.description: Lights up GitHub code-scanning UI. Largest scope.

questions.title: Exit code semantics
questions.context: CI integration depends on this.

options.label: 0 = no blockers (warnings allowed); 1 = blockers; 2 = validator error
options.description: Standard. Matches CONTEXT stub admission posture.

options.label: 0 = clean (no blockers AND no warnings); 1 = warnings; 2 = blockers; 3 = validator error
options.description: More granular; some CIs prefer.

options.label: Always 0 with JSON containing `gateOutcome`; CI parses JSON
options.description: Matches dark-factory output convention; requires CI tooling.
